name: ina-mays-guide-to-childbirth
description: Ina May Gaskin's classic guide — understanding Sphincter Law, the mind/body connection in labor, evidence-based birth choices, and reclaiming childbirth as empowering rather than medicalized.
useCase: Natural birth preparation, fear management, informed decision-making about interventions, VBAC planning, choosing a caregiver, creating a mother-friendly birth environment.
knownRisks: The Farm's approach is home-birth centered — hospital birth planning may look different
Some medical conditions genuinely require hospital birth — this skill is for low-risk pregnancies
The book was published in 2003 — some statistics and recommendations may be slightly outdated
